title: St Benedict's Church, Glastonbury

The Anglican **Church of St Benedict** at [Glastonbury](/wiki/Glastonbury "Glastonbury") within the English county of [Somerset](/wiki/Somerset "Somerset") was built as a Norman chapel in the 11th century with substantial additions in the 15th and 19th centuries. It is a Grade I [listed building](/wiki/Listed_building "Listed building").[[1]](#cite_note-nhle-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=St_Benedict%27s_Church,_Glastonbury&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The first church on the site was dedicated to [Saint Benignus](/wiki/Benignus_of_Armagh "Benignus of Armagh") in the 11th century, possibly due to confusion with the [Anglo-Saxon](/wiki/Anglo-Saxon "Anglo-Saxon") Beonna.[[2]](#cite_note-bho-2) The dedication was changed to [Saint Benedict](/wiki/Benedict_of_Nursia "Benedict of Nursia") in the 17th century.[[1]](#cite_note-nhle-1)

The current building is late medieval although the exact date is unknown. The tower was added in the mid 15th century.[[1]](#cite_note-nhle-1) Abbot [Richard Beere](/wiki/Richard_Beere "Richard Beere") (1493–1524) added the north [aisle](/wiki/Aisle "Aisle") and porch.[[1]](#cite_note-nhle-1) The [stained glass](/wiki/Stained_glass "Stained glass") windows were replaced in the 1840s.[[3]](#cite_note-3) In 1862 [Benjamin Ferrey](/wiki/Benjamin_Ferrey "Benjamin Ferrey") added the south chapel. [Victorian restoration](/wiki/Victorian_restoration "Victorian restoration") was carried out by [J. D. Sedding](/wiki/J._D._Sedding "J. D. Sedding") in the 1880s.[[1]](#cite_note-nhle-1) During 2014 extensive renovation work was carried out.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The church was a dependent chapel of the nearby [Church of St John the Baptist](/wiki/Church_of_St_John_the_Baptist,_Glastonbury "Church of St John the Baptist, Glastonbury") and both were under the control of [Glastonbury Abbey](/wiki/Glastonbury_Abbey "Glastonbury Abbey") until the [Dissolution of the Monasteries](/wiki/Dissolution_of_the_Monasteries "Dissolution of the Monasteries").[[1]](#cite_note-nhle-1) It is now part of a [benefice](/wiki/Benefice "Benefice") with St Johns and [St Mary's & All Saints Church](/wiki/Church_of_St_Mary,_Meare "Church of St Mary, Meare") in the village of [Meare](/wiki/Meare "Meare") within the [Diocese of Bath and Wells](/wiki/Diocese_of_Bath_and_Wells "Diocese of Bath and Wells").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Architecture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=St_Benedict%27s_Church,_Glastonbury&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Architecture")]

The [Lias](/wiki/Blue_Lias "Blue Lias") stone church has a four-[bay](/wiki/Bay_(architecture) "Bay (architecture)") [nave](/wiki/Nave "Nave"), north and south [aisles](/wiki/Aisle "Aisle"), a [chancel](/wiki/Chancel "Chancel") and a ar[clerestory](/wiki/Clerestory "Clerestory").[[1]](#cite_note-nhle-1) The three-stage west tower is supported by set back [buttresses](/wiki/Buttress "Buttress"). It has an embattled [parapet](/wiki/Parapet "Parapet").[[1]](#cite_note-nhle-1) It contains six bells, five of which date from 1776.[[2]](#cite_note-bho-2)

The interior includes a 13th-century [piscina](/wiki/Piscina "Piscina").[[1]](#cite_note-nhle-1)
